title: Volta Systems Closes $400M Series C in Landmark Quantum Computing Round
body:
Volta Systems, the Berlin-based quantum computing startup, announced Monday it has closed a $400 million Series C funding round led by Apex Ventures, with participation from Sequoia Capital and several sovereign wealth funds. The round values Volta at approximately $3.2 billion, making it one of Europe's most valuable deep-tech companies.

CEO Rolf Andersen called the raise "a validation of the commercial viability of error-corrected quantum hardware," adding that the bulk of the capital would go toward manufacturing scale-up at the company's new Dresden facility.

Apex Ventures managing partner Diana Chu said her firm had tracked Volta for two years before committing. "The team's approach to qubit coherence times is genuinely differentiated," she said. "We think this is a platform, not a product."

Volta plans to use the funding to expand its 280-person workforce by roughly a third over the next 18 months. The company did not disclose revenue figures but said it has signed letters of intent with three major pharmaceutical companies for drug discovery applications.
